insight: Germany's performance in the 2026 World Cup has shown a notable trend when it comes to hydration breaks, with the team gaining momentum in both of their breaks so far. This momentum gain is reflected in a mean increase of 0.250 points per minute, indicating a slight but significant improvement in their performance after the breaks. Notably, this gain has occurred when Germany was not pressing before the break, suggesting that the team is able to regroup and refocus during these pauses, allowing them to come back stronger and more cohesive. Overall, the data suggests that hydration breaks have had a positive impact on Germany's gameplay, providing them with a slight but potentially crucial advantage in their matches.
